note: Wave defined as one commit produced by the build agent — now explicit in Stage 4 flow
test_cmd config field added with empty-string skip behavior and auto-detection fallback
pre-build-sha.txt capture point defined in Stage 4; rollback uses $(cat queue/<slug>/pre-build-sha.txt)
Fatal defined as Verdict: FAIL; PASS WITH NOTES is explicitly non-fatal
Codex parsing contract: **High:** = blocking (1 fix attempt), **Medium:**/**Low:** = non-blocking (justification comment)
Invocation corrected: shell scripts call claude -p with autonomy-directive prepended; spike spec-review.sh first
run.sh sources ~/.zshenv.local at startup; LaunchAgent (not LaunchDaemon) requirement documented
Branch naming: autorun/<slug> from main; if branch exists reset to main; PR targets main
flock -n queue/.autorun.lock wraps run.sh; OS releases lock on any exit including crash
Accepted risk: all 7 run in parallel with no rate-limit handling; personal API key unlikely to hit limits — documented explicitly
Documented as known limitation: osascript best-effort; run-summary.md is reliable fallback; LaunchAgent required
Spec's halt rule wins over autonomy directive for fix-loop exit — no infinite loop, PR left open on failed fix
Stage 2 handoff mechanism still not explicitly defined — deferred to plan/implementation
PR provenance block still has no field template — deferred to plan/implementation
/spec --auto flag still an undeclared dependency; AC #7 retains it without sequencing note
Notification event triggers partially implied (halt/complete) but payload schema and per-event specifics not defined
